findings.signature: Bridge fix reverted: runnable bridge initializes memory DB directly in second process — exact corruption class the single-writer lease prevents
findings.notes: Broken-and-inert is safer than working-and-racy. The bridge stays inert until 028 code-index phase delivers IPC-backed transport. Any future code that touches the memory DB from outside the daemon lease MUST go through IPC, never direct init.

findings.signature: Lease PID accepted as owner only if alive AND real start time matches lease-recorded start time within 2s
findings.notes: Closes PID-recycling false-SKIP that masks crashes as healthy. Uses `ps -o lstart=`; falls back to liveness-only on unreadable start time. Any daemon lease validation must include start-time identity check.

findings.signature: Standalone system_code_graph MCP registration; ADR-001 Q3 (co-resident) explicitly superseded
findings.notes: Namespace changes from mcp__mk_spec_memory__code_graph_* to mcp__system_code_graph__*. Tool IDs remain stable. Path-flattening canonical: no mcp_server/code_graph/ intermediate. Any new MCP server follows this standalone topology pattern.

findings.signature: Code graph excludes .opencode/skills/** by default; 97% files/98.3% nodes were skill internals
findings.notes: Env SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_SKILLS=true or per-call includeSkills:true for opt-in. Per-call overrides env (ADR-002). Scope fingerprint stored in code_graph_metadata; requires full scan when stored and active scope differ. Any scan-default change must update the fingerprint and require explicit full scan.

findings.signature: Single index-scope.ts is the canonical source for memory/code-graph path exclusions; spec-doc filters are additive overlays only
findings.notes: Changing EXCLUDED_FOR_MEMORY automatically changes spec-doc classification and discovery. z_archive is intentionally NOT excluded (ARCHIVE_MULTIPLIERS 0.1 decay handles deprioritization). Any new exclusion must land in index-scope.ts, not scattered walkers.

findings.signature: Delete all z_future rows regardless of tier rather than downgrading; z_future must never be indexed
findings.notes: The invariant is 'z_future must never be indexed' — not 'lower priority'. Downgrading leaves forbidden rows searchable. Constitutional auto-surface was dominated by z_future research chunks before cleanup.

findings.signature: Invalid constitutional saves downgraded to important with governance_audit row; path must contain /constitutional/
findings.notes: Constitutional tier is path-gated: only files under /constitutional/ can be constitutional. Enforced at SQL write layer (ADR-007), not just handler layer. Any mutation surface (save, update, checkpoint restore, post-insert) must enforce this guard.

findings.signature: resolveCanonicalPath() prefers fs.realpathSync() to prevent symlinks from bypassing invariant enforcement
findings.notes: Symlinked files can bypass path-based exclusion checks if only path.resolve() is used. Falls back to caller-supplied path for missing/broken targets so atomic save flows still work.

findings.signature: /memory:save defaults to planner-first output (non-mutating); full-auto is explicit fallback only
findings.notes: Default path returns structured planner output instead of mutating. Reconsolidation-on-save (ADR-003) and post-insert enrichment (ADR-004) are opt-in only. Quality-loop auto-fix (ADR-006) removed from default path. Hard legality checks remain as blockers.

findings.signature: Model-keyed PREFIX_REGISTRY + env-var override replacing hardcoded Nomic prefix; empty string = no prefix
findings.notes: Two registries to keep in sync (TS HfLocalProvider + Python cocoindex). Legacy TASK_PREFIX export retained for back-compat. Empty string is a valid override (explicitly no prefix); undefined means fall through to registry. Wrong prefix silently degrades retrieval ~5-8%.

findings.signature: ONNX Runtime backend rejected: 5x slower on Apple Silicon at parity-preserving settings; sbert remains default
findings.notes: Neural Engine barely active for Gemma (23% ops, 77% CPU). Static-pad128 is fast but breaks parity (mean cosine 0.869). At parity-preserving settings ONNX is 3.5x slower. The ONNX code path is removed; rejected-experiment record preserved in packet.

findings.signature: invalidateEntityDensityCache() wired into post-commit branch of memory_save and memory_bulk_delete handlers
findings.notes: Call AFTER commit, not before — failed saves must NOT invalidate cache. Per-call overhead ~µs (in-process Map clear). Burst writes now propagate to entity-density signal immediately. Any handler that mutates memory_index rows must invalidate the cache post-commit.

findings.signature: SPECKIT_GRAPH_CHANNEL_PRESERVATION parsed as permissive boolean; '0'/'no'/'off'/'' now correctly disable
findings.notes: BREAKING for users setting =0 (they now actually get disabled). Default-ON preserved. Aligns with how every other SPECKIT_* flag is parsed. Any new SPECKIT_* env flag must use the same permissive boolean parser.

findings.signature: C+F hybrid manifest-driven greenfield: 86→15 source files; levels eliminated internally; single manifest drives scaffold+validator
findings.notes: Three orthogonal axes replace level scalar: kind + capabilities + presets. Single manifest (spec-kit-docs.json) drives scaffolder AND validator — drift structurally impossible. Inline section gates preserve markdown authoring. Follow-on packet implements this; 026 only designs it.

findings.signature: Level 1/2/3/3+ is the SOLE public/AI-facing vocabulary; preset/capability/kind are STRICTLY private
findings.notes: AI behavior + user conversation flow must remain byte-identical. Workflow-invariance CI test greps public surfaces for banned vocabulary. Internal vocabulary lives ONLY in manifest + resolver + shell wrapper. Any new feature that would require exposing preset/capability to users must add a new Level instead.

findings.signature: content-router.ts has documented scoped preservation exception for Tier 3 default-disable guard
findings.notes: Eight-category switch, Tier 1 logic, Tier 2 matching, and target selection are preserved. The documented exception is the Tier 3 default-disable and manual-review guard. Changelog and release notes must reflect this honestly.

findings.signature: Reddit post treated as primary-source field report with appropriate skepticism; findings passage-anchored
findings.notes: Every finding anchors to specific paragraph. Recommendation labels reflect evidence quality, not assumed validity. Structural conclusions stand independently of exact percentages. Any future research phase must preserve this source-framing discipline.

findings.signature: 926-vs-858 sessions and 18903-vs-11357 turns discrepancies preserved explicitly, never normalized
findings.notes: Smoothing introduces artificial precision the source doesn't support. NFR-D01 in spec.md makes preservation a non-functional requirement. Downstream planning can bound precision correctly. Any document referencing these counts must show both numbers.

findings.signature: Constitutional-tier guard enforced at SQL write layer; checkpoint restore validates rows inside transaction barrier
findings.notes: Handler-only checks leave blind spots for internal callers and restore paths. Restore rejection is all-or-nothing (atomic). Audit uses action='tier_downgrade_non_constitutional_path'. Any new mutation surface must go through the SQL-layer guard.

findings.signature: Code graph upgrades depend on packet 011 (validator owner) as hard predecessor; additive enrichment only
findings.notes: 011 owns fail-closed trust validation. 014 adds edge evidence classes, confidence backfill, detector provenance only after 011 stabilizes. Routing facade, Leiden clustering, GraphML/Cypher exports are prototype-later (ADR-003). Any graph enrichment must not compete with 011's trust-axis ownership.

findings.signature: Cleanup apply rebuilds its plan inside the transaction instead of trusting pre-transaction snapshot
findings.notes: Concurrent writes between plan generation and apply could leave violating rows behind. Dry-run output remains informative but the authoritative plan for mutation lives inside the transaction. Any cleanup/repair CLI must rebuild its plan inside the apply transaction.

findings.signature: Orphan launcher sweep was verified no-op: all 9 launcher parents are live sessions; launcher-lifecycle fix deferred to 028
findings.notes: Launchers ARE those sessions' MCP transports — killing them breaks working sessions. The real launcher-lifecycle fix for true owner-exit orphans stays with 028 skill-advisor workstream. Do not attempt to kill old launchers without parent-process classification.

findings.signature: Old structural-artifact bundle replaced by trust-axis separation: provenance, evidence status, freshness authority as separate fields
findings.notes: Contract before packaging: any downstream reader trying to treat cached startup, dashboard, or structural artifacts as ready-made defaults must be pointed back to trust-axis and freshness prerequisites first. R10 replacement is explicit in spec and ADRs.
